{{Short description|Assignment of a vector to each point in a subset of space}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Vector field
| image = VectorField.svg
| caption = A vector field representing wind velocity over a region.
| topics = [[Electric field]], [[Magnetic field]], [[Electromagnetism]], [[Fluid dynamics]], [[Gravitation]]
}}

In [[vector calculus]], a '''vector field''' is an assignment of a [[Vector (mathematics and physics)|vector]] to each point in a subset of [[space]]. Vector fields are often used in physics to model the [[speed]] and direction of a moving fluid throughout space, or the strength and direction of a [[force]], such as the [[magnetic field|magnetic]] or [[gravitational field|gravitational]] force.

== Definition ==
A vector field on a domain <math>D \subset \mathbb{R}^n</math> is a function <math>\mathbf{F}: D \rightarrow \mathbb{R}^n</math> that assigns to each point <math>\mathbf{x} \in D</math> a vector <math>\mathbf{F}(\mathbf{x})</math>:
:<math>\mathbf{F}(\mathbf{x}) = (F_1(x_1,\ldots,x_n), \ldots, F_n(x_1,\ldots,x_n))</math>

== Vector calculus operations ==

=== Divergence ===
The [[divergence]] measures the "outflow" of a vector field at a point:
:<math>\nabla \cdot \mathbf{F} = \frac{\partial F_x}{\partial x} + \frac{\partial F_y}{\partial y} + \frac{\partial F_z}{\partial z}</math>

[[Gauss's law]] relates divergence to flux through a closed surface.

=== Curl ===
The [[Curl (mathematics)|curl]] measures the "rotation" or circulation:
:<math>\nabla \times \mathbf{F} = \left(\frac{\partial F_z}{\partial y} - \frac{\partial F_y}{\partial z}, \frac{\partial F_x}{\partial z} - \frac{\partial F_z}{\partial x}, \frac{\partial F_y}{\partial x} - \frac{\partial F_x}{\partial y}\right)</math>

== Physical examples ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Field !! Type
|-
| [[Electric field]] || Gradient of scalar potential (electrostatic)
|-
| [[Magnetic field]] || Divergence-free (<math>\nabla \cdot \mathbf{B} = 0</math>)
|-
| [[Gravitational field]] || Conservative, <math>\mathbf{g} = -\nabla \phi</math>
|-
| Velocity field in a fluid || Describes fluid flow
|-
| [[Poynting vector]] || Energy flux of electromagnetic field
|}

== Conservative vector fields ==
A vector field is [[conservative force|conservative]] if it can be expressed as the [[gradient]] of a scalar potential:
:<math>\mathbf{F} = -\nabla U</math>

For a conservative field, the [[line integral]] is path-independent and the [[curvature|curl]] is zero.

== Uniqueness theorems ==
[[Helmholtz decomposition]]: any sufficiently smooth vector field can be decomposed into a curl-free (irrotational) and a divergence-free (solenoidal) component.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Electric field]]
* [[Gradient]]
* [[Magnetic field]]
* [[Scalar field]]
* [[Tensor field]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Vector calculus]]
[[Category:Mathematical physics]]
[[Category:Fluid dynamics]]
